channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Calgary Hit by Snowstorm, Resulting in Numerous Vehicle Accidents
item.description: Calgary experienced a disruptive spring snowstorm over the Easter weekend, leading to numerous vehicle crashes and challenging road conditions. The storm, characteristic of Alberta’s often unpredictab...

item.title: Tori Spelling and Seven Children Treated After Temecula Car Crash
item.description: Tori Spelling, the 52-year-old star of 'Beverly Hills, 90210,' was involved in a car accident in Temecula, California, while driving with seven children, including her four own children and three of t...

item.title: Electronic Artists Collaborate on Album to Support Endangered Bird Conservation
item.description: A new music project called Shika Shika is turning environmental awareness into sound. Founded by Robin Perkins, a UK-born musician and environmental campaigner known as El Búho, the initiative blends ...

item.title: Canada’s Growing Dependence on China: A Dangerous Shift in Foreign Policy
item.description: In a recent article, John Ivison criticizes Canada's increasing political and economic ties with China under the current Liberal government. While Canada’s Finance Minister, François-Philippe Champagn...

item.title: Jason Day Offers Sympathy for Tiger Woods Amid DUI Controversy
item.description: Australian golfer Jason Day has expressed both sympathy and confusion over Tiger Woods' recent decision to drive under the influence, an action that led to Woods being charged with a DUI after a car a...

item.title: NCERT Textbook Controversy: Academics Challenge Ban, Committee Formed
item.description: A significant controversy has erupted surrounding an NCERT (National Council of Educational Research and Training) textbook following criticism regarding a chapter discussing the judiciary and corrupt...

item.title: Border Patrol Nonprofits Selling Controversial Challenge Coins Raises Legal and Ethical Questions
item.description: US Border Patrol agents are selling challenge coins and other merchandise through nonprofit organizations that list Border Patrol facilities as their addresses. Some coins commemorate immigration enfo...

item.title: Court declares Kenyatta Hospital surcharge illegal, orders payment of withheld salary
item.description: The Employment and Labour Relations Court in Nairobi ruled in favour of a former supply chain assistant at Kenyatta National Hospital (KNH), blocking the hospital from imposing a KSh 3.5 million surch...

item.title: U.S. Stocks Edge Higher as Investors Await Trump’s Hormuz Shipping Decision
item.description: On April 6, 2026, major U.S. equity indexes experienced modest gains as investors closely monitored geopolitical developments in the Middle East and anticipated an approaching policy deadline from for...

item.title: Amit Jogi convicted and sentenced to life in 2003 murder case of Ramavatar Jaggi
item.description: The Chhattisgarh court has convicted former politician Amit Jogi and sentenced him to life imprisonment in connection with the murder of Ramavatar Jaggi that took place in 2003. The case, which had re...

item.description: Over the Easter weekend, a remarkable mass wedding ceremony took place at the International Pentecostal Holiness Church (IPHC), where His Grace Comforter FLG Modise solemnised the marriages of 58 coup...

item.title: DILG Implements Stricter Curfew and Public Drinking Ban in Metro Manila
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has announced stricter curfew regulations and a ban on public drinking and late-night karaoke sessions in Metro Manila. Interior Secretary Jo...

item.title: Michael Garron Hospital Adapts to Patient Surge, Converts Space for Care
item.description: Michael Garron Hospital in Toronto is facing an unprecedented surge in patients, far exceeding its original design capacity. Built to accommodate approximately 150 patients daily, the hospital now rou...

item.title: Gulf Desalination System Designed to Withstand Single-Point Disruptions
item.description: The Gulf region relies heavily on desalination for its freshwater supply, with countries like Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE producing the majority of their drinking water through this method. Desp...

item.title: Advance voting concludes in Terrebonne federal byelection amid potential impact on Liberal majority
item.description: On Monday, Terrebonne, Quebec saw the conclusion of advance voting in its federal byelection, which is taking place alongside two other byelections in Toronto. These elections are particularly signifi...

item.title: DJ Afro Explains Voice Change During Majembe vs Mbavu Destroyer Boxing Match
item.description: Kenya’s well-known movie commentator DJ Afro Amingos made a notable comeback at the recent Vurugu boxing event featuring Majembe and Mbavu Destroyer at Kasarani Indoor Arena. The fight went four round...

item.title: OpenAI Proposes Policy Measures to Mitigate AI-Driven Economic Disruption
item.description: OpenAI has outlined a set of policy proposals aimed at addressing the potential societal and economic disruptions caused by advanced artificial intelligence. The proposals include implementing taxes o...

item.title: Celebrating 50 Years of All The President’s Men: A Landmark in Investigative Journalism Cinema
item.description: All The President’s Men, directed by Alan J. Pakula and marking its 50th anniversary, remains an exceptional portrayal of investigative journalism. Based on the 1974 book by Bob Woodward and Carl Bern...

item.title: Edmunds Evaluates Geely Galaxy M9 SUV, Highlights Advanced Features and Competitiveness
item.description: US car-shopping platform Edmunds recently tested a Chinese-made SUV, the Geely Galaxy M9, marking its first evaluation of a vehicle from China. Despite Chinese cars being effectively banned in the Uni...

item.title: Egypt’s energy-saving curfew reshapes Cairo’s nightlife and economy
item.description: Egypt’s government has instituted new early-closing orders requiring most shops, restaurants, and malls in Cairo to shut by 9pm on weekdays and 10pm on weekends. The measure, in effect since April 3, ...
